---
title: "Karolina Nugumanova"
image: profile_2.jpg
about: 
  template: trestles
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 16em
  links:
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/karolina-nugumanova-0b4917130/
    - icon: cloud-fill
      text: Bluesky
      href: https://bsky.app/profile/karolinanugumanova.bsky.social
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/Karolina-Nugumanova
    - icon: bank
      text: University Page
      href: https://www.sns.it/en/persona/karolina-nugumanova
    - icon: database-fill
      text: Web of Science
      href: https://www.webofscience.com/wos/author/record/OEP-1093-2025
    - icon: mortarboard-fill
      text: Google Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?hl=en&user=JBryBecAAAAJ
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:kar.nugumanova@gmail.com
---
At the intersection of political sociology, gender, and migration, my research investigates the emotional, gendered, and structural dynamics of political mobilization under high-risk environments and exile. Combining large-$N$ panel surveys, experimental designs, and in-depth qualitative fieldwork, my work uncovers how gender and emotions shape resistance and political voice after exit.

Currently, I am a Visiting Research Fellow at the Faculty of Human Sciences at Waseda University (invited by Prof. Naoto Higuchi) and the Institute of Social Science at the University of Tokyo (invited by Prof. Masaaki Higashijima). In January 2027, I will join the Davis Center for Russian and Eurasian Studies at Harvard University as a Fellow. I am also completing my PhD in Political and Social Sciences at the [Scuola Normale Superiore](https://www.sns.it/it/persona/karolina-nugumanova) in Florence, under the supervision of Prof. Martin Portos and Prof. Lorenzo Zamponi.

As a Co-Investigator of [*OutRush*](https://outrush.io/eng)---a landmark global panel study tracking over $30{,}000$ post-2022 Russian emigrants---our project findings have been widely featured by international outlets including *The New York Times, Financial Times, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, BBC, and Meduza*. I also serve as a Co-Investigator for *Democracy in Exile* (DemEx), a project funded by the National Science Foundation (USA), and lead [*The Science of Dreams*]((https://readymag.com/u94255285/wardreams/2/)), an innovative study capturing wartime dream narratives to analyze collective emotion and identity. Previously, I served as an expert and foresight facilitator for the European Commission.

My scholarly articles and public-facing analysis have appeared in peer-reviewed journals, analytical platforms and independent media such as *Communist and Post-Communist Studies*, *The February Journal*, the *Russian Analytical Digest*, *Republic*, *Paper*, and *Posle.media*. Beyond my research, I am a regular commentator and podcast guest on Russian and international media outlets, including *Meduza* and *SBS Radio*. I hold an MA and a BA in Sociology from the [European University at St.~Petersburg](https://eusp.org/en) and [Novosibirsk State University](https://english.nsu.ru/}).
